task: I need to answer a company’s follow-up about a billing issue, and I want the reply to be clear and easy to use. Can you help me draft something I can send without a lot of back-and-forth?

prefs.explanation: She is unlikely to appreciate hedging or soft-pedaling when she asks for help. Her life pattern is to cut through confusion quickly and verify what matters, so direct answers fit her style. Overly delicate wording would probably feel evasive or wasteful to her.

prefs.short: Prefers concise answers over long explanations
prefs.explanation: She tends to act on practical information and would likely prefer the core point first. Long-winded responses would feel like clutter unless she specifically asks for detail. She seems to value efficiency in communication the same way she values efficiency in paperwork and routines.

prefs.short: Likes organized checklists and clear steps
prefs.explanation: She responds best to information that is easy to verify and follow in order. A structured format would match her record-keeping habits and reduce the chance of missing something important. Loose, rambling prose would be less useful to her than a clean list or step-by-step outline.

She is not looking for warmth that feels performative or sentimental. A steady, neutral tone would likely feel more trustworthy because it avoids trying to manipulate her mood. She probably dislikes being treated as fragile or emotionally steered.

She is very protective of her personal space and information, so she would likely want interactions that do not pry. She would probably be uncomfortable with unnecessary requests for private details or casual data-sharing. A restrained approach that asks only for what is needed would fit her well.

turns.agent_response:
Yes — here’s a short, firm reply you can send:

> I dispute this billing issue and do not agree to any changes or charges. Please provide the exact basis for the billing in writing, including the dates, amounts, and any supporting documents. Until I receive that information and agree in writing, no changes or charges are authorized.

If you want, I can also make it:
- more firm,
- more polite, or
- tailored to email vs. letter.

per_pref.reason: Blunt and direct: "I dispute this billing issue" and "no changes or charges are authorized".
Concise and to the point; it gives a ready-to-send draft without extra explanation.
Well organized and easy to use, with a quoted draft and a short bullet list of optional tweaks.
Matter-of-fact, neutral tone throughout; no warmth or emotional language.
Minimal disclosure: it asks only for "the exact basis for the billing in writing, including the dates, amounts, and any supporting documents" and avoids unnecessary personal details.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a tighter version you can send:

> I dispute this invoice as currently billed. Please send a corrected invoice or a line-by-line breakdown showing the basis for each charge, including dates, amounts, and supporting documentation. I will not discuss payment until I receive that information. All further communication on this issue must be in writing.

If you want, I can also make it a little firmer or turn it into a shorter email subject + body.

per_pref.reason: Very direct and blunt: "I dispute this invoice as currently billed" and "I will not discuss payment until I receive that information." No hedging or softening.
Concise and efficient. It gives a ready-to-send draft in a few sentences and says "here’s a tighter version you can send."

Well organized and easy to follow, with a clear request list: "corrected invoice or a line-by-line breakdown" and specifics like "dates, amounts, and supporting documentation." Not a full checklist, but structured.
Matter-of-fact and neutral. The wording is firm but not emotional or sentimental, e.g. "I dispute this invoice" and "All further communication... must be in writing."
Minimal disclosure. It asks only for what is needed and avoids prying into personal details; it specifically limits contact to "in writing" and requests billing documentation only.
